harness: For each case: run skills/job-preference-interview/evals/files/setup-interview-ws.sh <tmp>; export JOBSEARCH_OS_REGISTRY=<tmp>/registry.json and JOBSEARCH_OS_HOME=<tmp> (so the brief is written under <tmp>/.job-search, never real data). Invoke the job-preference-interview skill. When the skill asks questions, answer with the canned answers in the prompt. Then inspect <tmp>/.job-search/preferences.md. HARNESS CAPABILITIES (P7): a DISCOVERY scenario (discovery:true) plants this skill among the siblings it names and drives a NAIVE prompt (no skill named); it passes only if the runner SELECTS must_select and not any must_not_select sibling. Discovery is behavioral, so it is stochastic:true — run it `reps` times, record pass-rate + variance (scripts/eval_harness.py aggregate_reps), and run its no-guidance control arm (description withheld) so the green result is attributable to the trigger description, not the base model (control_delta). The interview verdict-shape cases (1-3) are single-shot STRUCTURAL checks (sections present, no numbers, import preserved) and stay single-shot. The N>=5 reps are the OFF-CI live step (AAS-T-10); CI gates the structural coherence only.

evals.prompt: Invoke as an orchestrator would, with a depth-bearing args string (args: 'write the brief — standard interview'); the depth ask must still be offered. Canned answers: depth='standard'; situation='employed, passively looking'; role='senior/staff AI engineer, individual contributor, NOT management'; domain='LLM products at a startup'; location='remote in the US, or SF Bay onsite; onsite elsewhere is a dealbreaker'; comp='wants meaningful equity; base flexible'; wlb='no heavy on-call'; stack='Python + modern LLM tooling'; dealbreakers='primarily people-management; onsite outside SF'. When asked to calibrate, keep it qualitative. Finish the interview.
expectations: Offers the depth choice (Quick sketch ~1 / Standard ~6–10 / Thorough ~15–20) BEFORE any interview question — even though the invocation args named a depth — as a multiple-choice question (the question box; numbered prose fallback only if the tool is unavailable)

The depth question's lead is phrased naturally (introduces the brief once, in the agent's own words) and is NOT a verbatim recital of a canned definition; the option labels (Quick sketch / Standard / Thorough) are the fixed menu
Asks questions ONE at a time (not a single long dump)
Writes <tmp>/.job-search/preferences.md containing all five sections: Summary, Must-haves / dealbreakers, Strong preferences, Nice-to-haves, Red flags, plus a created_at line
Items are concrete/observable prose (e.g. 'Remote within the US, or SF Bay onsite')
Contains NO numeric score, NO 0–100 rubric, NO category weights, NO scoring formula
The IC-not-management and onsite-location dealbreakers appear under Must-haves / dealbreakers
Ends with a 'How to use this' line stating Claude judges relevant/not + weak/moderate/strong with reasoning and 'No score.'
Ends by showing the written brief in the reply as rendered markdown (not inside a code fence), with a one-line note of where it's saved

evals.prompt: User says 'I already have a brief' and provides the contents of evals/files/imported-brief.md. Do not run a full interview.
expectations: Skips the full interview
Writes <tmp>/.job-search/preferences.md preserving the user's prose (sections intact)
Adds/keeps a created_at line; output has no numeric scoring

evals.scenario: import a brief that contains a 0–100 rubric
evals.prompt: User provides evals/files/imported-rubric-brief.md (which includes a 0–100 scoring rubric and per-category weights).
expectations: Notes that this system judges qualitatively and converts the brief to prose, dropping the numeric rubric/weights
The written preferences.md contains the prose sections and NO 0–100 rubric and NO weights
Writes <tmp>/.job-search/preferences.md with all five sections (Summary, Must-haves / dealbreakers, Strong preferences, Nice-to-haves, Red flags) and a created_at line

control.strip: Present the same naive prompt with job-preference-interview's trigger description withheld (only the sibling descriptions visible).
control.expectation: With the description present the runner routes 'figure out what I want' to the interview; the no-guidance control mis-routes to evaluate-job-fit (which judges an existing posting) at a measurably higher rate (control_delta > 0).

evals.prompt: A user with no posting in hand and no skill named says: 'I want to figure out what I actually want in my next job before I start looking.' The sibling skills evaluate-job-fit (judges a specific posting against a brief) and job-search (home/onboarding) are installed alongside job-preference-interview.
expectations: job-preference-interview is SELECTED — the user wants to BUILD the preferences brief, which is exactly this skill's job
evaluate-job-fit is NOT invoked — there is no posting to judge; the user is defining preferences, not testing a role against them
Offers the depth choice (quick sketch / standard / thorough) and asks questions one at a time; the written brief carries no numeric scoring

evals.scenario: quick-sketch depth — one-sentence brief, high-signal, no padding
evals.prompt: Invoke the job-preference-interview skill standalone. At the depth choice, the user picks 'Quick sketch' (or says 'just a quick sketch — I want to see jobs fast'). To the one sketch question they answer with a single sentence: 'Senior backend engineer, fully remote in the US, base at least ~$180K, and no on-call.' They share nothing else. Finish.
expectations: Offers the depth choice (Quick sketch ~1 / Standard ~6–10 / Thorough ~15–20) as a closed choice BEFORE any question, then on Quick sketch asks a SINGLE question and takes whatever the user gives

Drafts <tmp>/.job-search/preferences.md with the five sections (Summary, Must-haves / dealbreakers, Strong preferences, Nice-to-haves, Red flags) and a created_at line, from ONLY what the user said: the role, remote-in-US, and the ~$180K base floor become Must-haves; 'no on-call' becomes a Red flag
Does NOT invent preferences the user never expressed — thin sections are left sparse rather than padded, and the brief can be deepened later
Contains NO numeric score, NO 0–100 rubric, NO category weights, NO scoring formula (the '$180K base' floor is captured as plain prose, not math)

Ends with a 'How to use this' line stating the assistant judges relevant/not + weak/moderate/strong with reasoning and 'No score.', shows the brief rendered in the reply (not inside a code fence), and notes in one line where it was saved
